content:
fool-MASC.SING.2p.PAT


idk how to describe it
Barakaj
Shankshahonungshasha
Can anyone help with autobenefactives and how they work?
What’s so weird about them? I just have dative-verb agreement

Oh no I mistook this.

In my language masturbation always takes the autobenefactive. Others benefactive exists but it means doing it in front of someone else for their gratification 

It always is auto-instrumental
Shankshahonungshashka
I do masturbate with autobenefactive but I did it to be funny not naturalistic

ˈʝʌːʁʊskjə̌ˌmɑ̃̀ːn

I think this but the phonology would change in other ways to fit Aelaki
Aelaki means "all the words of heaven"

A lot of people who don't know the language will say

"Aelaki is really pronounced Alaku"

Because they don't get that the name of the langauge is specifically in a collective plural
adsr_onvy

Everyone's name adapted into Aelaki has a reverse umlaut done on it so that the grammar works

I am Kamudoro

You are

Straboru Poptarts
henry>3p.MASC.GEN.SING< dog>3p.POSSESSED.MASC.SING<-3p.MASC.SING.PAT HOD-lost-3p.MASC.SING-AUD.PAST himiko-3p.FEM.SING.SPEAK SPEAK_AUX.DUB.3p.FEM.SING

Honrushag Kashavangosh Goyazangoshro Humukoshro Oshoshoro

Honrug's Dog is lost according to Humuko (doubting their reliability)

(Honrug = Henry, Humuko = Himiko both Aelakized)
You can also use the auxiliary verb agreeing to yourself to indicate doubt in your account.

The auxiliary verb construction is mandatory in some expressions.

"I heard there was an explosion yesterday" requires the auxiliary verb to indicate that someone told you there was an explosion. Otherwise it is ambiguous between you heard the explosion (I hope your hearing is intact) vs someone told you about an explosion
So you can have a phrase like "Bob told me that the sky is blue" as one clause, because Bob is in the speaker case
When you are using an auditory evidentiality, the speaker indicates who told you it. You can use an auxiliary verb to indicate the gender/number/person of the speaker without declining them.
Apparently the speaker class is something I entirely created on my own which no natlang has. But I am just going with it anyways.
Did I show you the class system I decided to implement?
Okay. I was thinking it was a variant of the Dative, where you would do it so "I danced for her" would have her in the experiencer case.
I wanted to ask how your experiencer case worked
@geek2000 is that correct?
Do you know what he changed his username to? I wanted to ask him about his case system
@deglaciation are you Melanated Geek?
You write songs in conlangs right?
Because I am interested in whether your songs are done in a particular register or something like that
Is there a role channel that I don’t know about or did you have to ask mods?
Do you construct songs?
@llythyrynmud what does your music theorist rule mean?

This might have been a situation to use the fourth person in, but I feel like using an obviate on a king especially the current one is disrespectful, and like it violates some level of topic coherence too
SVO but only in times of ambiguity.
Normally, the language has a relatively fluid word order, but this was an oddball sentence with many people of the same gender so it needed to adopt a strict archaic word order to explain everything
So yeah, I got this sentence and it used a surprisingly large amount of grammatical structures

To say this mostly with loanwords 

Rühüngush Shashabechekushkashrom ka Bazhashya Kühüngash nono Ëkutashashë Kühüngash Nühofuglon 

Damn everyone being masculine made this harder than I was expecting
Right now, I can get the syntax immediately, but I don’t remember any of those words if I have them at all
Who is doing the giving?

I am Superman

Gotharoth zukoth Supermanath Zikath Thayahahag. 

The list is made and established as one noun phrase, and then I use the word stand  which is kind of a dummy verb I use to turn it into a real sentence because without that verb, it sounds like an incomplete sentence like “me aka superman…”

I have a lot of weirdness with the word to be. It is generally a pretty difficult thing to explain that one thing is identical to the other. Because it relies on listing two or more discrete things and then referring to them as a group. 

Lots of languages have to phrase things in complicated ways to say something that would be simple in another language. And that is a pretty cool thing because it means that different ideas become more obvious to some people speaking some language languages than others.
@ektura. If you think this conversation was helpful, you might be able to get your point across a bit better now.
That’s great. I’m very happy that I helped you.
Sorry late response
Wait, why? I think this is an amazing argument for not having a word for each other.

Well, it does in terms of not having the word, but if this word order was always read as reciprocal then the language might use reciprocal a lot because of the fact that it is so easy and you don’t need to remember a relatively uncommon word

If you did this then

Jack Jill and Eat 

Will always seem off.

And in this case, you would use the phrasing 

Jack Jill and Food Eat 

Because you need the object that is eaten to prevent the verb from being read as reciprocal
So if you need to say something like each other, and don’t have a word for that, then couldn’t you use this ambiguous sentence to mean that in all contexts?
But do you get that even in English that is an ambiguous sentence?
Jack and Jill fight can either mean Jack and Jill fight each other or Jack and Jill fight on the same side against somebody else
English already has this as something of a construct in it, and it already means this to some extent
You could add more to the sentence, but didn’t you make verbs work this way for a reason?
Jack Jill and Fight makes a bit more sense with this meaning.
Obviously, this will be a bit more context dependent, but I think this could be an interesting way to express that
So if it is ambiguous, whether Jack and Jill are eating or being eaten in that sentence. Doesn’t it make sense that they could be eating each other?
And is not a verb it is a conjunction
Jack Jill and eat is ambiguous about whether the two of them are eating or being eaten right?
My idea was that because the language is Ambitransitive there is an ambiguity when you have a pair, followed by a verb
Oh, that makes sense so is that how you say themselves or each other?
I am a bit confused because I gave two different sentences
I’m not sure where the Jack Jill and Hug came from. Is this the order that the language uses for and?
So was that you reading it as a valid sentence in English and translating it?

I am kind of confused about what you were saying here and I realize that what I gave was a bad example because Jack and Jill Hug is actually a valid sentence in English while I thought that it was nonsense
I am sorry I am having some trouble with opening that document
oh locative only but not marking subject or object?
Isn’t it obvious on each noun, which one is eating and which one is being eaten?
if you have cases then how are your verbs ambitransitive? Isn’t one of them marked as the object and the other as the subject all the time?

Jack and Jill Hug is a rather simple thing I thought of. 

Jack Jill Hug means that Jack Hugged Jill 

But the and creates a bit of ambiguity. Are the two of them separate arguments or is the noun phrase of both of them, the subject of the verb?
Because when you only have one noun in the clause it is ambiguous due to the word order. Why not play around with that for phrases like this?

My way of doing it is one that I don’t think that is compatible with your language. But something I think is especially interesting about your language is if you have no cases and an SOV order you could have a very long list of people who then do something to each other.
Did you see the way that I phrased those sentences without having a word like that?
I don’t think that most languages have that word because it refers to something rather specific
Sorry, I think you are being a bit too harsh and trying to hammer down this point without letting him talk about his language in ways that would make the point a bit more apparent.
I think SOV actually makes a lot of sense for being ambitransitive.
Btw did you figure out “themselves” in your language?
SVO?
So what is the word order in the language?
Yeah, a lot of things in English are very clearly different from other languages in their phrasing
Oh, that’s what what you’re saying
I feel like this might be an independent kind of morphosyntactic alignment
Do you know of any languages that are like this?
Yeah, it would be pretty worrying. If every single time my friends said they were going to go eat. I had to be afraid that a monster is gonna eat them or something.
4 arguments all the same and all mandatory
Honestly, I am very proud of that word that I made earlier today the sheer repetitiveness of the word and vagueness of what it means without context always makes me laugh when I say it
Yeah correct
I sent her a letter
Some verbs are ditransitive so they have three nouns
Do you know what a subject and an object are?
Yeah, but transitive verbs have a subject and an object
So your conlang is active accusative i presume?

The way I do it is that because the language is so pro drop you can usually drop whichever one is less important for a simpler sentence that’s focused on one thing. But stative verbs in particular have some operations that can be done with them such as the gnomic and inchoative that mean that you will want to find one of these words, even if it is relatively obscure.
I guess it broke kind of feels like pseudo ergativity.
Yeah, these are pretty different things. At least what I do in my language is you just simply can’t even do this because each verb is pretty rigid in terms of the arguments that it can get.
Why did you move chats?
